title: 10 best herbal teas for cracked heels

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cracked heels, promoting a faster healing process.

Moisturizing: The gel extracted from aloe vera leaves contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help lock in moisture and soften the skin, reducing cracking and flaking.
Antibacterial: Aloe vera's antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can infect cracked heels, preventing further complications and promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with cracked heels because of its potent moisturizing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium. These nutrients help to deeply nourish and hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and inflammation associated with cracked heels. Regular consumption of aloe vera tea can also help to promote collagen production and improve skin elasticity, ultimately leading to healthier and smoother heels.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex sugars help to lock in moisture, soothing dry skin and promoting the healing of cracked heels.
Glucomannans: These soluble fibers help to soften and hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of cracks and promoting skin elasticity.

Vitamins and minerals (such as vitamin E, vitamin C, and zinc): These antioxidants and essential nutrients help to protect the skin from damage, promote collagen production, and support the overall health and integrity of the skin, reducing the severity of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their high gel content, which provides soothing and moisturizing properties to help heal cracked heels.
Buds: Aloe vera buds are used to make teas because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with cracked heels.
Flowers: Aloe vera flowers are used to make teas for their antiseptic properties, which aid in preventing infection and promoting wound healing in cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-12 aloe vera leaves from mature plants with thick green skin.
Cut the leaves in half lengthwise and scoop out the gel-like substance inside.
Soak 1-2 teaspoons of the aloe vera gel in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining pulp.
Serve the tea immediately and store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

Saponins: Saponins in licorice tea are known for their moisturizing and emollient properties, which can help lock in moisture and soften dry, cracked skin on the heels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with cracked heels because of its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to moisturize and soften the skin, reducing dryness and cracking in the heels.

Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with cracked heels.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds possess antiseptic and antioxidant properties, which help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate cracked heels, while also promoting collagen synthesis and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make teas for cracked heels because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and soften skin.
Leaves: Used to make teas for cracked heels because they possess antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can aid in the healing of cracked skin.
Roots: Used to make teas for cracked heels because they contain saponins, which can help to moisturize and hydrate dry, cracked skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the dried roots in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids completely.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before serving or refrigerating.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours and serve chilled or at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 fresh calendula flowers when in full bloom and dry them in a cool dark place for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 2 to 3 teaspoons of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Steep the powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately and enjoy its potential benefits for skin and eye health.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 cups of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers with cold water to remove dirt and debris quickly.
Chop the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into small pieces to release their medicinal properties immediately.
Steep 1 teaspoon of taraxacum officinale per cup of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to infuse the tea.

Chop the fresh herb into small pieces or crush the dried herb to release its active compounds immediately.
Combine the chopped fresh herb with 250ml of boiling water or steep the dried herb in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herb particles and liquid immediately.
Discard the herb particles and serve the symphytum officinale tea warm or at room temperature to drink.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in St John's Wort tea have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help prevent infections that can worsen cracked heels and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
Barks: Containing salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to soften and exfoliate dry, cracked skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried hypericum perforatum leaves according to traditional methods to ensure potency.
Measure one teaspoon of dried hypericum perforatum leaves and add it to one cup boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool and drink it three times a day, ideally on an empty stomach, for optimal absorption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with cracked heels because its antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm dry, cracked skin. The tea's antiseptic qualities also work to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, allowing for faster healing of cracks. Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's antioxidant compounds help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking of the skin on the heels.

Seeds: Rosemary seeds are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants which help to moisturize and nourish the skin, promoting healthy foot care and relief from cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves measuring 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan or kettle to a rolling boil for 10 minutes.
Steep the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container taking 2-3 minutes.
Allow the herbal tea to cool down to room temperature before applying it topically as a foot soak for 20 minutes.

Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to repair and regenerate skin cells, promoting healthy foot skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lemongrass leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add one tablespoon of coconut oil to enhance moisturizing properties and soothe dry skin.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before applying it topically to cracked heels.

remedy_recipe: Rinse 2 teaspoons of dried turmeric root powder into a heat-resistant cup with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the turmeric to infuse its bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste and mix well as desired for sweetness and soothing properties.
Allow the tea to cool to lukewarm temperature before applying it topically to cracked heels.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea root powder measuring 2 teaspoons for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the harvested echinacea powder with the boiling water in a medium-sized heat-resistant glass bowl.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reaches a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth discarding the solids and retaining the flavorful liquid.
Consume the cooled tea once daily as needed up to a maximum of three cups per day for optimal results within 6-8 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with cracked heels because its natural ingredients provide an effective remedy for dry, cracked skin. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in herbal tea can help to soften and moisturize the skin, reducing flakiness and peeling. Barks: With its astringent properties, barks can help to reduce moisture loss and soothe irritated skin, making it an effective treatment for dry, cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality green tea leaves and grind them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground tea with two cups of boiling water to create a soothing brew.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the antioxidants and flavonoids to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the loose particles and sediment.
Drink one cup of the warm tea three times daily for up to two weeks to promote healthy skin hydration.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cracked heels that are treated with herbal teas are dry skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint are often used to soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and itchiness. Additionally, herbal teas rich in antioxidants, such as green tea and ginger, can help to combat premature aging and improve skin elasticity, promoting healthy and hydrated skin.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cracked heels are a natural remedy that helps to moisturize and soothe dry, cracked skin on the heels. These teas are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that promote healing and hydration. The best herbal teas for cracked heels include Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melaleuca alternifolia, Urtica dioica, Avena sativa, Aloe vera, Plantago lanceolata, Aloe ferox, and Rosmarinus officinalis. If left untreated, cracked heels can lead to pain, discomfort, and difficulty walking, affecting daily activities and overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with cracked heels is a blend of Epsom salt tea and peppermint tea, combined with calendula tea and comfrey tea. Epsom salt tea softens and moisturizes the skin, while peppermint tea cools and soothes the cracked areas. Calendula tea promotes skin healing and reduces inflammation, while comfrey tea nourishes and repairs damaged skin cells. This combination helps to hydrate, calm, and repair the skin, promoting healthy and smooth heels.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas helps with cracked heels because of its hydrating and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help to lock moisture into the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe vera also reduce redness and pain associated with cracked heels, promoting healing and softening of the skin. Regular consumption of Aloe vera tea can lead to healthier and more radiant skin on the feet.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin, which in turn helps to soothe and repair cracked heels.
Glycoproteins: These compounds aid in the regeneration of skin cells and the repair of damaged skin tissues, leading to improved skin health and reduced cracking.
Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm irritated skin and reduce pain associated with cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize dry, cracked heels.
Gel from Leaves: The aloe vera gel extracted from the leaves is often used to make soothing teas for cracked heels due to its ability to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation.
Pulp from Leaves: The pulp from the leaves is high in fiber, vitamins, and minerals, making it a popular ingredient in teas for cracked heels, as it helps to soften and nourish dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 aloe barbadensis leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Cut the aloe leaves into small pieces and blend them in a blender with 1 cup water for 1 minute.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids for 10 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon honey and 1/2 teaspoon lemon juice to the liquid and stir well for 30 seconds.
Pour the herbal tea into a cup and serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas helps with cracked heels because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea, rich in flavonoids and triterpenoids, accelerates the healing process of damaged skin by promoting collagen production and reducing redness. Additionally, calendula's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture, softening and hydrating the skin. As a result, cracked heels begin to mend, and the skin becomes smoother and more resilient, reducing the appearance of cracked and dry skin. This natural remedy provides a soothing and nourishing solution for dry, cracked heels.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help to moisturize and soften the skin, reducing the dryness and cracking associated with cracked heels.
Tannins: Contribute to the anti-inflammatory properties of Calendula, reducing redness and swelling in cracked heels, while also helping to protect the skin from further irritation.
Phenolic acids: Act as antioxidants, promoting skin health and reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to skin dryness and cracking in the heels.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and soften skin.
Leaves: They are said to have astringent and antiseptic properties, which can aid in healing and protecting cracked skin.
Roots: They are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny day in mid-summer.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 days.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried calendula flowers for every 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their properties.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with cracked heels because of its moisturizing and soothing properties. The saponins present in the roots of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant have anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce swelling and irritation associated with dry, cracked skin. The tea's emollient properties also help to lock in moisture, promoting the healing and softening of cracked skin. Regular consumption of this tea can help to restore the natural barrier function of the skin, leaving heels feeling smooth and healthy.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with cracked heels by reducing inflammation and promoting skin cell regeneration due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Liquiritigenin: Contributes to the healing of cracked heels by modulating the skin's natural barrier function and reducing oxidative stress, thereby promoting skin health and hydration.

Isoliquiritigenin: Assists in the treatment of cracked heels by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of collagen, which helps to improve skin elasticity and reduce dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which help in soothing and hydrating dry cracked heels.
Rhyzomes: Used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which aid in healing and protecting cracked heels.
Leaves: Employed for their astringent and antiseptic qualities, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of cracked skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root from a trusted supplier or your garden.
Rinse the dried root with cold water to remove any impurities or debris.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Add the rinsed root to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas helps with cracked heels because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tea, made from the leaves of the Melaleuca tree, contains compounds that promote healthy skin growth and regeneration. When applied topically, the tea's active ingredients can help soothe and moisturize dry, cracked skin, reducing inflammation and promoting the formation of new skin cells. remedy_constituents: Cineole (also known as eucalyptol): Helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of cracked skin by exhibiting antimicrobial properties.
Terpinen-4-ol: Helps to moisturize and soften cracked skin by exhibiting emollient and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in the repair and hydration of dry skin.

Caffeic acid: Helps to protect the skin from damage and promote the healing of cracked heels by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for cracked heels due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: They are used to make teas for cracked heels due to their ability to soothe and moisturize dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves or 20g of dried leaves from the plant's leaves.
Steam 250ml of water in a tea pot for 5-10 minutes to prepare for the herbal tea.
Combine the steamed water with 1-2 teaspoons of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the melaleuca alternifolia leaves before serving the tea hot.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica teas helps with cracked heels because it contains natural compounds that promote skin health and hydration. Stems: They are used to make teas because of their rich content of vitamins and minerals, which can help to nourish and moisturize dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried urtica dioica leaves and stems in late spring or early summer when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica leaves and stems in a warm dark place for several days to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine one teaspoon of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems with one cup of boiling water to make a tea.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the urtica dioica to release its active compounds into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving it hot or cold to enjoy its medicinal benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa teas helps with cracked heels because it is rich in nutrients and antioxidants that promote skin health and hydration. The oat kernel extract found in Avena sativa stimulates collagen production, reducing inflammation and soothing dry, cracked skin. Fatty acids: Fatty acids in Avena sativa, particularly oleic and linoleic acids, have emollient and moisturizing properties that can help to soften and hydrate dry, cracked skin on the heels.

Leaves: Avena sativa leaves are used to make teas that can help to soften and hydrate dry skin, reducing the appearance of cracked heels.
Roots: The roots of Avena sativa are used to make teas that are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin on the heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried avena sativa flowers and leaves for the tea.
Rinse the harvested avena sativa with filtered water to remove impurities instantly.
Steep one teaspoon of dried avena sativa in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the herbs completely.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature for consumption immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera teas helps with cracked heels because it contains nutrients and antioxidants that promote skin health and hydration. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera soothe and calm dry, cracked skin, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture, allowing the skin to heal and regenerate more efficiently. Anthraquinones: Have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort caused by cracked heels.
Vitamins (especially Vitamin E and Vitamin C): Act as antioxidants, promoting wound healing and collagen synthesis, which helps to repair and strengthen the skin on cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Aloe vera for making teas, as they contain high amounts of gel and other nutrients that help to soothe and moisturize cracked heels.
Stems: Aloe vera stems contain a higher concentration of aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that aid in healing and softening cracked skin.
Rhyzomes: Aloe vera rhyzomes, or underground stems, contain aloe-β-barberin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing effects that help to repair and protect cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel from the plant's leaves and store it in the refrigerator for later use.
Combine 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel with 4 cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes or until the aloe vera gel is fully dissolved.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove any remaining solids.
Store the herbal aloe vera tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata teas helps with cracked heels because it promotes skin regeneration and hydration. The tea, rich in mucilages, acts as a natural emollient to soften and moisturize dry, cracked skin. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm irritation. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthier and more resilient skin barrier. This makes it an effective remedy for treating cracked heels naturally.

Triterpenoids: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with cracked heels, promoting healing and comfort.
Phenolic acids: Exhibits antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and preventing infection in cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and dry out cracked heels.

Seeds: Seeds are used for their moisturizing and emollient properties, which help to hydrate and soften the skin of cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantago lanceolata leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated to ensure freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried plantago lanceolata leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose herbs.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe ferox teas helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the skin. Aloemodin: Contributes by preventing the bacterial growth that can infect cracked heels, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to repair itself.
Anthraquinones: Helps by acting as a natural humectant, retaining moisture in the skin and preventing further dryness that can exacerbate cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe ferox leaves are rich in soothing and moisturizing properties that help to soften and heal cracked heels.
Gel from Leaves: The gel inside Aloe ferox leaves is used to create a hydrating and protective barrier on the skin, helping to repair and moisturize cracked heels.
(If only 2 are required) Leaves' sap: Aloe ferox leaves' sap, also known as the gel, contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing compounds that help to soothe and heal cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 aloe ferox leaves from mature plants with thick green skin and yellowish green spines.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in 4 cups of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a large container to remove the solids and pulp.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to sweeten the tea and mix well to dissolve the honey completely.
Serve the aloe ferox tea hot or chilled after it has cooled down to room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas helps with cracked heels because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and healing dry skin. The tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and promote cell regeneration, ultimately leading to healthier skin. Additionally, the tea's hydrating properties help to lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of cracks and promoting a smooth, soft skin texture. remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Helps to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the recovery of cracked heels.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin from damage, promote collagen production, and improve skin elasticity, reducing the appearance of cracked heels.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used to make teas due to their high antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and moisturize dry skin.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning when they are at their highest oil content level.
Dry the harvested rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the rosmarinus officinalis tea and serve hot or allow it to cool before refrigerating.
